measure.CatchLine: Reduces discount for on-time payment of property taxes imposed on business property.
measure.MeasureSummary:
Reduces discount for on-time payment of property taxes imposed on business property. Reduces amounts scheduled for distribution by tax collector to taxing jurisdictions to provide funding for certain tax administration programs. Directs portion of interest and charges on delinquent property taxes to be distributed to taxing units. Amends statutes to provide funding mechanism for administration of property tax by counties. 
	Creates Assessment and Taxation Improvement Grant Program to provide grants for county projects for improved property tax administration. 
	Requires planning division of municipality issuing building permit to notify county assessor, electronically if possible, of information, including tax lot number, required to be contained in permit. 
	Takes effect on 91st day following adjournment sine die.
measure.RelatingToFull: Relating to property tax administration; creating new provisions; amending ORS 294.175, 294.178, 294.184, 294.187, 311.390, 311.392, 311.505 and 311.508; and prescribing an effective date.
measure.RelatingTo: Relating to property tax administration; prescribing an effective date.
measure.AtTheRequestOf: (at the request of Governor Kate Brown for Department of Revenue)
measure.FiscalImpact: May have fiscal impact, but no statement yet issued
measure.RevenueImpact: May have revenue impact, but no statement yet issued

sponsors.PresessionFiledMessage: Introduced and printed pursuant to House Rule 12.00.  Presession filed

historyActions.ActionText: First reading. Referred to Speaker's desk.
